dimensions.name: Problem Clarity
dimensions.level_1: Problem is absent, vague, or stated only as a solution wish. No observable symptom.
dimensions.level_3: Problem is described but uses abstract language or soft metrics. Symptom is identifiable but not precisely bounded.
dimensions.level_5: Problem is stated as a concrete, observable symptom with a measurable gap. No solution language present.

dimensions.name: Stakeholder Specificity
dimensions.level_1: No stakeholder mentioned or stakeholder is generic ('users').
dimensions.level_3: Stakeholder role identified but motivation or impact unclear.
dimensions.level_5: Stakeholder role named with clear motivation and why this issue matters now.

dimensions.name: Problem/Solution Separation
dimensions.level_1: Brief proposes technical implementation details: table names, class names, gem choices, schema structure.
dimensions.level_3: Brief mostly stays in problem space but one or two solution-space leaks exist.
dimensions.level_5: Brief contains zero solution-space details. No class names, table columns, or implementation mechanisms.

dimensions.name: Success Criteria Observability
dimensions.level_1: Success criteria absent or only reference code artifacts (tests, migrations, class presence).
dimensions.level_3: Success criteria describe system behavior but include at least one undefined term or solution-space detail.
dimensions.level_5: Each success criterion is expressed as a verifiable system behavior or domain outcome with no undefined terms.

dimensions.name: Scope Boundary Integrity
dimensions.level_1: Out-of-scope section is absent or excludes something required to fix the stated problem.
dimensions.level_3: Out-of-scope section exists but is incomplete or uses vague boundaries.
dimensions.level_5: Out-of-scope section explicitly lists related but excluded areas without conflicting with the success criteria.
